kp8:1.1: A person stashes a fund away
kp8:1.2: deep underground, at the water line:
kp8:1.4: this will provide for my needs,
kp8:2.1: for my release if I’m denounced by the king,
kp8:2.3: in case of debt, famine, or accidents.”
kp8:2.5: With aims like this in the world
kp8:2.6: a reserve fund is stashed away.
kp8:3.1: But no matter how well it’s stored,
kp8:3.2: deep underground, at the water line,
kp8:3.3: it won’t all always serve one’s need.
kp8:4.1: The fund gets shifted from its place,
kp8:4.2:
or one’s memory gets confused;
or—unseen—
kp8:5.1: or hateful heirs run off with it.
kp8:6.1: But when a man or woman has laid aside a well-stored fund of generosity, virtue, restraint, & self-control,
kp8:7.1: with regard to a shrine, the Saṅgha,
kp8:8.2:
It can’t be wrested away.
It follows you along.
kp8:8.3: When, having left this world, for wherever you must go,
kp8:9.1: This fund is not held in common with others,
kp8:9.3: So, enlightened, you should make merit,
kp8:9.4: the fund that will follow you along.
kp8:10.2: that gives all they want to beings human, divine.
kp8:10.4: that is all gained by this.
kp8:11.4: That is all gained by this.
kp8:12.3: kingship over devas in the heavens:
kp8:12.4: That is all gained by this.
kp8:13.1: The attainment of the human state,
kp8:13.4: That is all gained by this.
kp8:14.4: That is all gained by this.
kp8:15.2:
the perfection of disciplehood:
That is all gained by this.
kp8:15.4: That is all gained by this.
kp8:16.3: Thus the wise, the enlightened, praise the fund of merit
